rules.description: Always identify the higher timeframe (daily) bias before analyzing intraday setups
A market structure shift (MSS) requires a candle close beyond the last swing high/low, not just a wick
Fair Value Gaps are only valid if formed with displacement (strong impulsive move), not choppy price action
Equal highs/lows are liquidity targets — price is drawn to them before reversing
Check DXY direction when analyzing forex pairs — risk assets (indices, crypto) typically inversely correlate with DXY
Asian range defines liquidity for London session — London often sweeps Asian highs or lows before the true move
During high-impact news (NFP, CPI, FOMC), flag setups as high-risk and note the event

Gold (XAU) correlation analysis: rising gold with falling indices = risk-off confirmation, but rising gold with rising indices = currency debasement/inflation signal. Context determines interpretation
Bitcoin dominance matters — if BTC rises while altcoins fall, sentiment is defensive within crypto
If fewer than 2 confluences align for a setup, classify confidence as low (<40). Never force a trade narrative.

When using causal language (due to, because of, driven by) or making cross-asset attribution statements, populate assumptions array with unverified claims. Then develop minimum two equiprobable explanations: 'Primary thesis: [explanation A] with [evidence]. Alternative thesis: [explanation B] with [evidence]. Probability assessment: [A%]/[B%].' Both explanations must receive equal analytical development.

When analyzing cross-asset divergences, use proxy measures if primary data unavailable (e.g. if DXY missing, reference VIX, 10Y yields, sector rotation, or currency basket performance). Always attempt magnitude assessment using available data, state which proxy measures were used, and quantify the divergence relative to historical norms or provide directional context.
For each setup, include the timeframe context and what invalidates the thesis. Avoid setups that contradict the broader macro thesis without strong technical conviction.

Confidence methodology: (1) Technical confluence: 1=20%, 2=35%, 3=45%, 4+=55%. (2) Macro alignment: 40-60% for strong correlation, 20-40% for weak correlation, 0-20% for breakdown. (3) RR clarity: +10% for RR>2.5, +5% for 1.5-2.5, -5% for <1.5. (4) Volatility adjustment: reduce by 10% during VIX>25. (5) Calibration cap: superseded by r031 which sets the active hard cap at 65% with mandatory notation — do not add a separate 50% cap. Analytics show 30-50% range well-calibrated (45% hit rate) while 50-70% range severely overconfident (21% hit rate). NOTE: displayed confidence is system-calibrated — do not modify this rule to compensate for calibration adjustments.

Mixed bias requires quantified justification meeting one condition: (1) equal-weighted conflicting signals across 3+ asset classes with magnitude differences >1.5%, (2) correlation breakdown with proxy data showing >1 standard deviation from norm, (3) VIX >25 with risk assets rallying, (4) coordination magnitude exceeding 2x typical ranges across 4+ asset classes creating uncorrelated opportunities, (5) weekend crypto sector divergence >1.0% between infrastructure tokens (BNB, SOL) and utility tokens (XRP, ADA, LINK) indicating defensive rotation patterns. Document specific divergence magnitude and historical context.
Classify price moves quantitatively: small (<1 SD), normal (1-2 SD), large (2-3 SD), extreme (>3 SD). If historical data unavailable, state 'magnitude context unavailable'.
rules.disabledReason: Requires historical candle data (Phase 1 of roadmap) — re-enable when OHLCV data is available

rules.description: Every setup must include: (1) specific timeframe, (2) entry level with 0.5-1% buffer from current price for volatility, (3) clear invalidation point, (4) risk/reward ratio >1.3 and <4.0. Setups with RR <1.3 or >4.0 must be classified as 'incomplete' - ratios exceeding 4.0 indicate calculation errors requiring immediate correction.
Before completing any session, verify presence of: confidence breakdown (r014), complete setup specs (r017). Flag missing elements in the reflection.

When primary data unavailable (DXY, VIX, etc.), automatically deploy r012 proxy measures in this order: (1) currency cross-rates, (2) sector rotation, (3) yield differentials, (4) volatility proxies. State which proxy used and confidence level.
rules.disabledReason: Meta-rule that just references r012 — creates self-criticism loops without adding value

rules.description: When stating moves as 'normal', 'large', or 'extreme' per r016, attempt to provide statistical anchor: either reference recent X-day volatility ranges, compare to typical session ranges for that instrument, or state 'historical context unavailable' if data insufficient. Never use magnitude descriptors in isolation.
rules.disabledReason: Depends on r016 which requires historical candle data — re-enable when Phase 1 is implemented

rules.description: For coordinated moves exceeding 2% across 3+ asset classes, activate exceptional conditions analysis: (1) quantify coordination magnitude, (2) distinguish single-catalyst vs multi-factor environments, (3) MANDATORY: systematically screen all 5 asset classes for structural opportunities regardless of analysis complexity - coordination magnitude demands screening magnitude, (4) flag regime-change potential if coordination persists >2 sessions. Under-delivery relative to identified coordination level constitutes execution failure.

Technical confluence scoring with mandatory ICT classification: count confluences from (1) trend alignment with higher TF, (2) confirmed structure break, (3) liquidity level interaction, (4) volume/momentum confirmation, (5) key psychological level, (6) fair value gap alignment, (7) order block interaction. Apply scoring: 1=20%, 2=35%, 3=50%, 4+=65%. Classification mandatory: confluence (2) = MSS, confluence (3) = Liquidity Sweep, confluence (6) = FVG, confluence (7) = OB. Default to 'Other' ONLY when none of these confluences present.

Confidence scores must reference specific measurable criteria: technical confluence based on counted confluences per r022, macro alignment quantified by correlation coefficients or divergence magnitude, RR clarity by actual ratio values. No arbitrary percentages.

When identifying correlation breakdown, provide quantitative context: compare current divergence to 30-day average correlation, note if exceeding 1-2 standard deviations, or reference specific threshold levels (e.g. VIX >25 with equities +0.5% = moderate breakdown, VIX >30 with equities +1% = severe breakdown).

When confidence exceeds 65% with clear directional bias, systematically identify and evaluate minimum 3 structural levels across different instruments, documenting specific price levels and rejection rationale. Must produce minimum 1 setup unless all 3 levels explicitly rejected for quantified reasons (poor RR <1.3, stop distance >2%, conflicting timeframes). High confidence without documented level evaluation indicates systematic failure.

When confidence exceeds 60%, must produce minimum 4 setups across 3+ asset classes OR provide comprehensive screening validation documenting specific rejection reasons for minimum 8 instruments across forex/indices/crypto/metals/commodities. Each rejection must specify quantified rationale: poor RR <1.3, stop distance >2%, or conflicting timeframes. Systematic under-delivery relative to confidence level indicates execution discipline failure requiring immediate behavioral correction.

When confidence exceeds 70% with clear directional bias, systematically screen each asset class (forex majors, indices, crypto, metals, commodities) for structural levels aligned with bias direction. Document which instruments were screened and why setups were/weren't identified.

When confidence >35% with mixed bias or correlation breakdown affecting 3+ asset classes, systematically evaluate ALL five asset classes for independent structural opportunities: (1) Forex majors (7 pairs), (2) Indices (4 instruments), (3) Crypto (8+ instruments), (4) Metals (2 instruments), (5) Commodities (2 instruments). Mixed bias scenarios require comprehensive screening since correlation breakdown creates uncorrelated opportunities. Document which specific instruments were evaluated within

Stop distance validation with volatility context: when any instrument individually moves ≥5% in session, that specific instrument requires stop ≥1.5% from entry. When moves ≥3% but <5%, requires stop ≥1.0% from entry. No exceptions during exceptional coordination - elevated volatility demands wider stops regardless of correlation. Validation must occur before setup completion, not after session end.

Weekend sessions: ALL 10 crypto instruments (BTC, ETH, BNB, XRP, SOL, DOGE, ADA, LINK, DOT, AVAX) require evaluation. Each must produce either (a) complete setup with entry/stop/target/RR/TF, or (b) explicit key level with reasoning ('poor RR', 'conflicting timeframes', 'insufficient confluence'). Instruments showing >0.5% moves or mentioned in analysis text without corresponding setup/level constitute validation failures requiring immediate correction.

Confidence scores exceeding 65% indicate overconfidence bias activation. When calculation exceeds 65%, document the technical/macro factors driving higher score, then cap output at 65% with notation 'capped from [X]% due to calibration discipline'.

Mixed bias scenarios with systematic cross-asset screening warrant higher confidence scoring. Analytics show 56% hit rate in mixed calls versus much lower directional accuracy. When producing 4+ quality setups across 3+ asset classes during correlation breakdown, apply +15% confidence adjustment to base technical/macro scoring.

Weekend screening: when instrument shows positive performance >0.5% aligned with sector thesis, MUST produce either (a) specific entry setup with all required fields, or (b) explicit key level with rationale why no setup viable. Text mention without setup/level = compliance violation.

Screening accountability: when confidence exceeds 50% with identified bias, must document evaluation of minimum 3 specific structural levels (support/resistance, liquidity zones, fair value gaps) across different instruments. If no setups generated from 3+ level evaluations, explicitly state why each level was rejected (poor RR, high volatility risk, or conflicting timeframes).

Bearish bias requires: (1) minimum 2 consecutive sessions of confirmed lower highs AND lower lows across primary instrument, (2) correlation alignment (VIX rising while risk assets fall), (3) distribution volume pattern confirmation. Without all three criteria, default to mixed bias regardless of short-term bearish signals

Before classifying any setup as bearish direction, ALL must be confirmed: (1) Higher timeframe shows 2+ consecutive lower highs AND lower lows, (2) Confirmed MSS to downside (candle close below swing low, not wick), (3) DXY not in active weakness (if EUR/USD, GBP/USD, AUD/USD all up >1%, no bearish risk asset setups), (4) Overall session bias is bearish or mixed-bearish. If conditions unmet, classify as neutral or omit.

Weekend crypto screening validation: after completing analysis text, systematically verify each of the 10 crypto instruments (BTC, ETH, BNB, XRP, SOL, DOGE, ADA, LINK, DOT, AVAX) has either (1) complete setup with entry/stop/target/RR/TF, or (2) explicit key level with reasoning. Any instrument mentioned in analysis text without corresponding setup/level constitutes compliance violation requiring immediate correction before session completion.

High-conviction validation: when confidence exceeds 60% with identified directional bias, final output must contain either (1) minimum 2 setups across different asset classes, or (2) documented evaluation of specific structural levels in minimum 5 instruments with explicit rejection reasons. Sessions failing this validation require immediate rule review and execution process enhancement.

When confidence exceeds 55% during coordinated market moves (3+ asset classes moving >2%), screening validation requires either (1) minimum 2 setups across different asset classes, or (2) explicit documented rejection of specific structural levels in minimum 3 asset classes with quantified reasoning (poor RR <1.3, stop distance >2%, conflicting timeframes).

Before finalizing any session, verify confidence score in analysis text matches JSON confidence field within 5 points. If mismatch detected, use analysis text value as authoritative and document discrepancy reason.
rules.disabledReason: Superseded by resolveConfidence() in validate.ts (PR #63) which programmatically corrects text/JSON confidence mismatch

rules.description: Setup pre-validation gate: before constructing any bearish risk asset setup (indices, crypto), verify EUR/USD and GBP/USD are not both +1% or higher. If both exceed +1%, skip bearish risk asset construction regardless of technical levels. Document skipped levels with reason 'USD weakness constraint per r036'.
rules.disabledReason: Superseded by filterR036Setups() in validate.ts (PR #64) which hard-removes bearish risk asset setups during DXY weakness

rules.description: Weekend crypto sessions with sector divergence >1.0% require structural context analysis: document order flow patterns (rejection levels, volume clusters), historical precedent for current divergence magnitude, and catalyst assessment (regulatory, institutional, or technical drivers). Superficial percentage moves without structural context constitute depth violations.

When confluence factors include trend alignment, structure breaks, or key levels, classify as specific ICT patterns: trend+structure = MSS, liquidity+key levels = Liquidity Sweep, gap alignment = FVG, rejection zones = OB. 'Other' only when genuinely no ICT confluence present.

When market structure across primary instruments shows <0.5% change and key levels remain untested from previous session, explicitly state 'Market structure unchanged from session #X — analysis deferred' rather than reproducing identical technical commentary. Reserve full analysis for genuine structural evolution.

When cross-asset price levels and correlation patterns match previous session within 0.3% tolerance and no key structural levels have been broken, state 'Market structure largely unchanged from session #X - analysis deferred' rather than reproducing identical technical commentary. Reserve full cross-asset analysis for genuine structural evolution or new catalyst emergence.
